objective: Reduce batch execution from 10h53min to <5min (target: 10x improvement)

reader.status: OK — only reads, no business logic

processor.status: BOTTLENECK — O(rows × categories) with reflection overhead

writer.status: ACCEPTABLE for batch size, but serialized per chunk (no async)

connection_pool.status: Fixed (commit 4978e0c), but max-pool=3 may limit parallelism

bottleneck_analysis.primary: Reflection overhead in processor (getFieldValue per column)
bottleneck_analysis.secondary: Network latency AWS↔Locaweb per chunk (50s per SQL execute)
bottleneck_analysis.tertiary: Chunk size 50 may be suboptimal (limits throughput)
not_bottleneck: Memory (1.3k objects per chunk is tiny)
Pool exhaustion (max-pool=3 is small but sufficient for serial chunking)

recommendations.title: Eliminate Reflection in Processor
recommendations.description: Replace getFieldValue() reflection with direct field access via generated code or method references

implementation_details.current: Method.getMethod(getterName) + invoke() — called 3.1k times per chunk
implementation_details.proposed: Replace with MethodHandle or code-gen to access CsvEstatisticaRow fields directly
impact: 3.1k calls per chunk × 0.01-0.02s = 31-62s saved per chunk
implementation_details.effort: Medium (requires refactor of field access, maybe Lombok/code-gen)
implementation_details.testing: All 22 unit tests must pass, plus spot-check CSV parsing

recommendations.title: Increase Chunk Size from 50 to 500
recommendations.description: Trade memory for throughput by reducing overhead per-chunk (number of SQL execs, commits, context switches)

implementation_details.proposed: chunk-size: 500 → 25 chunks, but batch write becomes 13k items per SQL
impact: Reduces chunk-management overhead (fewer SQL calls, fewer commits)
Per-chunk SQL network latency becomes amortized over 10x items
Increases heap memory for processor results (~650 objects in-flight vs 1.3k) — still < 10MB
Estimated delta: -40% (-261s per chunk? No — bottleneck is write latency, not chunk count)
More realistic: -15% (-98 min total) due to fewer context switches
implementation_details.risk: Low (HikariCP pool may see more concurrent load, but max=3 is conservative)
implementation_details.testing: Run integration test with chunk-size=500, verify write order & idempotence

recommendations.title: Reduce Network Latency via Batching or Async Writes
recommendations.description: The 50s per SQL call is dominated by AWS↔Locaweb latency (~100-300ms per round-trip). Use connection multiplexing or async writes

implementation_details.current: JdbcBatchItemWriter (synchronous, 1 SQL per chunk, blocks on write)
implementation_details.proposed_a: Async ItemWriter (Spring Batch 5+ supports async item writer) — queue writes, batch on callback
implementation_details.proposed_b: JDBC MultiRow INSERT via stored procedure (batch 13k rows into single exec plan)
implementation_details.proposed_c: HikariCP max-pool-size: 3→5 + parallel chunks (requires step redesign)
implementation_details.impact_a: Async can pipeline chunks (execute SQL N while processing N+1)
implementation_details.impact_b: Stored proc may reduce latency if Locaweb supports it
implementation_details.impact_c: Parallel chunks with pool=5 could give 2-3x throughput, but complicates state
implementation_details.effort: High (requires async ItemWriter + redesign of chunk flow)
implementation_details.risk: Medium (async failure handling, thread safety, idempotence must be verified)
implementation_details.testing: Integration test with async + retry logic, verify no lost writes

phase_3_polish.change: Parallel chunks (if async doesn't hit target)

optimization_roadmap.note: Fixes 1+2+3 combined should reach ~3.25h (still >5min target). Parallel chunks (Fix 4) may be needed for <5min

quick_wins_low_risk.reason: Causes false positives during chunk processing (60s threshold < processing time). Already noted in incident report.
quick_wins_low_risk.impact: Reduces log spam, improves GC pressure

quick_wins_low_risk.change: Verify HikariCP config is optimal for concurrent access
quick_wins_low_risk.reason: max-pool=3 is conservative (sufficient for serial chunks), but if async writes added, may bottleneck

next_steps: 1. Measure baseline locally: Run batch with Docker MySQL + sample CSV, record time breakdown
4. Design Fix 3 (async) with retry logic, implement + measure delta
5. If <5min target not met after 3 fixes, implement parallel chunks
constraints: All changes must maintain idempotence (UPSERT must work correctly)
No changes to schema or FK logic
Must remain compatible with roubometro-back (same DB)
Performance test must use production-like data (12.4k CSV rows, 323k writes)
